steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: A NodeBalancer is a traffic cop at the front door of your building. Routes visitors to the right tenant. Doesn't care which apartment is full — just keeps people moving.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
NodeBalancers are managed L4/L7 load balancers. Features:

- Public IP assigned automatically
- TCP, HTTP, HTTPS protocols
- Active health checks (HTTP path or TCP connect)
- Sticky sessions (cookie or table-based)
- TLS termination with bring-your-own cert
- $10/month flat per balancer

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: On LKE, you don't create them by hand. Set Service type=LoadBalancer in your manifest and the cloud-controller-manager provisions one. For non-K8s workloads (raw Linodes, Linode Marketplace apps), use linode-cli nodebalancers create.
"NodeBalancer = managed LB. On LKE it's automatic. Off LKE it's one CLI call."

quiz.question: You set type=LoadBalancer on an LKE Service. What happens?

options.text: Nothing — you must manually create one
options.explain: LKE auto-provisions it via the cloud-controller-manager.

options.text: A NodeBalancer is auto-provisioned with a public IP
options.explain: The CCM watches for type=LoadBalancer Services and creates a NodeBalancer routing to your pod IPs.

options.text: A static IP is reserved but not bound to anything
options.explain: The IP is bound to the new NodeBalancer immediately.
type=LoadBalancer always provisions an external balancer.

quiz.question: Where is TLS terminated for HTTPS traffic on a NodeBalancer?

options.explain: You can configure either: TLS at the NB, or pass-through to the pod.

options.text: At the NodeBalancer if you upload a cert; otherwise pass-through
options.explain: Configure the NB with a cert + key for HTTPS termination, or use TCP mode for pass-through to your origin.

options.text: Only at the Akamai CDN — never the NB
options.explain: CDN termination is separate. NBs handle their own TLS.
